hypothesis: The exact clean baseline is not executable in this controller because `no_change` actions are policy-rejected, so the least-deviating Round 2 move is a single-variable `topk` learning-rate probe around the reference recipe. A modest LR increase from `8e-4` to `1.2e-3` may improve convergence speed and end-of-run FVU at `K=128` without changing the family, optimizer, or LUTurbo compatibility path.
summary: Keep the mainline `topk` family and the full reference recipe fixed, changing only `LR` from `8e-4` to `1.2e-3` at `K=128, EXPANSION_FACTOR=12, OPTIMIZER=signum`. This is the smallest policy-valid follow-up after the rejected clean-baseline attempt and should answer one concrete question: whether the current signum topk anchor is mildly under-stepped.
self_review: This stays on the same family, respects the single-variable rule, and follows the recommended search order as closely as the controller allows after rejecting `no_change`. The main risk is that we still lack a true baseline curve, so interpretation must compare curve shape against the reference recipe expectation rather than against an actual completed `LR=8e-4` run.
notes_to_memory: The controller rejects exact clean-baseline submissions when encoded as `no_change`, so mainline progress must proceed through a policy-valid single-axis variant.
Round 2 keeps `topk` fixed and starts the recommended LR stage with a modest upward step from the reference `8e-4` recipe.
Judge this run by curve shape as well as final FVU: smoother early descent with continued late improvement would support staying on the higher-LR branch.
next_hypotheses: If `LR=1.2e-3` gives a smoother or better curve, try one more upward LR step on the same `topk` recipe before changing optimizer.
If the curve becomes noisy or degrades late, step down below the reference LR on the same family and optimizer.
If LR tuning looks exhausted without a clear gain, keep `topk` fixed and move to the next optimizer as the next single-variable question.
